zh.Booton, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可布托
zh.Poynton, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England: 英國爾馬可萊斯費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport於1343年出生在英國爾波易頓時，他的父親Sir Edward de Warenne29歲，母親Cicely de Eton32歲。他於1371年與Margaret Stafford在英國諾廷加姆爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1386年11月25日在英國英格蘭諾福爾可布托享年43歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可。

zh-hans.Booton, Norfolk, England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可布托
zh-hans.Poynton, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England: 英国爾馬可萊斯費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport于1343年出生在英国爾波易頓时，他的父亲Sir Edward de Warenne29岁，母亲Cicely de Eton32岁。他于1371年与Margaret Stafford在英国諾廷加姆爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1386年11月25日在英国英格兰諾福爾可布托享年43岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Poynton
meanings: Anglo-Norman: Poynt, a point, a stop, and tun, a house, meaning a pointhouse, a business place

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Stockport
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Stopford  .

variants.name: Stopford
meanings: English: locative name from Stockport (Cheshire), which is recorded as Stokeport in 1188, Stockford in 1283–8, Stopford in 1347, Stoppord in 1337, and Stapporte in 1583.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport was born in 1343, in Poynton, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir Edward de Warenne, was 29 and his mother, Cicely de Eton, was 32. He married Margaret Stafford in 1371, in Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 25 November 1386, in Booton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport est né en 1343, à Poynton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward de Warenne, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Cicely d’Eton, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Stafford en 1371, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 25 novembre 1386, à Booton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport 1343 in Poynton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Edward de Warenne 29 und seine Mutter Cicely de Eton 32. Er hat 1371 in Nottinghamshire, England Margaret Stafford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 25. November 1386 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Booton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport è nato il nel 1343 nel Poynton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Edward de Warenne, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Cicely de Eton, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Stafford nel 1371, nel Nottinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 25 novembre 1386, nel Booton, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockportが1343年にイギリスチェシュアイアポイントンで生まれた時，父Sir Edward de Warenneは29歳，母Cicely de Etonは32歳であった。1371年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでMargaret Staffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1386年11月25日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクボオトンで43歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport(이)가 1343년 영국 체실르 포이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edward de Warenne(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Cicely de Eton(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1371년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 Margaret Stafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1386년 11월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 부턴에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport nasceu em 1343, em Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edward de Warenne, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Cicely de Eton, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Stafford em 1371, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de novembro de 1386, em Booton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport родился в 1343 г. в местности поинтон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Edward de Warenne было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Cicely de Eton, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Stafford в 1371 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 25 ноября 1386 г. в местности бутон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport nació en 1343, en Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edward de Warenne, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Cicely de Eton, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Margaret Stafford en 1371, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 25 de noviembre de 1386, en Booton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Norfolk, Inglaterra.
